title: Timing diagram — your register loads D when Load=1, holds when Load=0
setup:
# --- plot helper (hidden) — a register timing diagram driven by reg_bit ---
# Steps a Load signal and a D input through the student's own reg_bit and plots
# three stacked square waves: Load, D, and Q. Q should follow D while Load=1 and
# hold its value while Load=0. If reg_bit isn't ready, Q stays flat and says so.
def __steps(vals, base):
    pts = []
    for t, v in enumerate(vals):
        pts.append([t, base + v])
        pts.append([t + 1, base + v])
    return pts

def __wave():
    N = 8
    load = [t % 2 for t in range(N)]         # 0,1,0,1,... load pulses
    d_in = [1, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1]
    rb = globals().get("reg_bit")
    ok = rb is not None
    q = 0
    q_series = []
    for t in range(N):
        if ok:
            try:
                q = rb(d_in[t], load[t], q)
            except Exception:
                ok = False
                q = 0
            if q not in (0, 1):
                ok = False
                q = 0
        q_series.append(q if ok else 0)
    return [
        {"name": "Load", "points": __steps(load, 4), "highlight": False},
        {"name": "D (input)", "points": __steps(d_in, 2), "highlight": False},
        {"name": ("Q (your register)" if ok else "Q (write reg_bit to see it)"), "points": __steps(q_series, 0), "highlight": True},
    ]
